title: useInterval that doesn't drift and respects tab visibility
scenario_context: Polling for updates every 30s. Naive setInterval drifts under tab throttling and runs in background tabs (wasted).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: setInterval in useEffect with [] deps — handler stale
what_breaks_naive_approach: Handler captures initial state; never sees latest props.
reasoning: Ref for handler. Effect sets interval. Visibility event pauses.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-091, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Pause on hidden saves CPU. 2) Ref handler always fresh. 3) delay=null pauses semantically.

title: useToggle and useBoolean with semantic methods
scenario_context: Sidebar open/closed, modal show/hide. Naive useState<boolean> + setShow(!show) lies in async paths.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
what_breaks_naive_approach: Multiple async toggles race; final state wrong.
reasoning: useCallback with functional setter. Methods: toggle, on, off.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-092, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Functional setter avoids stale. 2) Semantic methods. 3) Stable identity.

title: useHotkey for keyboard shortcuts with scope and modifier
scenario_context: App-wide shortcuts (cmd+k for command palette, esc to close). Conflict with input focus. Need scope-aware handler.
verification_chain: Layer 9: ignores when typing in input unless allowInInputs
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Listening on every component — multiple firings
Hardcoded ctrl vs meta — Mac/PC issue
No preventDefault — browser shortcut fires too
what_breaks_naive_approach: User types 'k' in search field; cmd palette opens unexpectedly.
reasoning: Parse combo: 'mod+k' = cmd on Mac, ctrl on Win. Check target tagName for inputs. preventDefault on match.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Conflict between hotkeys — last attached wins, or use registry
test_strategy: Press combo with input focused — no fire. Outside input — fires.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Mac/Win mod abstraction. 2) Input scope respected. 3) Combo parser supports all modifiers.

title: useWindowSize with throttled updates and SSR safety
scenario_context: Conditional rendering based on viewport size. Naive: resize handler on every pixel.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
what_breaks_naive_approach: Component reads window.innerWidth in render → SSR mismatch.
reasoning: useState + resize listener + rAF throttle. SSR returns null/defaults.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for generic_react_hooks; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: SSR null fallback
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: generic_react_hooks_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-094, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) rAF throttle aligns with paint. 2) SSR null forces consumer to handle. 3) Cleanup.

title: Error code system documented and grep-able
scenario_context: Many error codes accumulate. Devs need single source listing all codes with meaning. Code search reveals usage.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Typo 'QUOTAA_EXCEEDED' on server; client checks 'QUOTA_EXCEEDED' — no match.
reasoning: const ERROR_CODES = { QUOTA_EXCEEDED: 'QUOTA_EXCEEDED', ...} as const. TS literal types. Single import.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Constants only
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Switch on ERROR_CODES.X

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Constants > strings. 2) Searchable. 3) Single source.

title: Toast notifications with queue, priority, and accessibility
scenario_context: Toasts pile up during burst. Need queue, max visible, priority ordering, aria-live for SR.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic dismiss timing — errors should persist longer
what_breaks_naive_approach: 10 errors in 2s — 10 toasts stack and overflow.
reasoning: Queue: incoming sorted by priority. Display max 3 (highest). Auto-dismiss with timer based on length + severity. aria-live='polite' for info, 'assertive' for errors.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for error_handling_client; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Calls toast.error

test_strategy: Burst 5 toasts, assert 3 visible + 2 queued. Dismiss → next appears.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Queue + max visible. 2) aria-live for SR. 3) Action button for recovery.

title: Strict null checks across layers; absent is meaningful
scenario_context: Optional vs nullable vs missing — distinct semantics. Zod handles all three.
verification_chain: Layer 5: schema distinguishes z.optional vs z.nullable
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Coalescing null to undefined — loses 'cleared' intent
JSON has no undefined — must use null
what_breaks_naive_approach: User clears nickname; backend sends undefined; old code treats as 'not provided' instead of cleared.
reasoning: undefined = not provided; null = explicitly cleared. Zod schema explicit. Form sends null to clear.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Standard
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: end_to_end_type_safety_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

edge_cases_addressed: JSON serialization — null preserved, undefined dropped
Mongo update with null vs unset
observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-097, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Semantic distinction. 2) Explicit in schema. 3) UI affordances for clearing.

title: Tenant deletion with cascading cleanup and grace period
scenario_context: Tenant requests deletion. GDPR requires data removal. Need: 30-day grace, full cascade, audit trail.
verification_chain: Layer 3: cascade deletes per related model
Layer 8: user can restore within grace
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Async cascade with no completion check
what_breaks_naive_approach: User regrets deletion same day — irrecoverable.
reasoning: deletedAt set; tenant blocked from login but readable to support. Background job 30 days later purges all data. Restore unsets deletedAt within window.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Standard
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.

test_strategy: Request → tenant readable, login blocked. Restore within grace. After grace — purged.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Grace period. 2) Cascade complete. 3) GDPR-compliant final purge.

title: Revenue events for analytics: MRR, churn, expansion
scenario_context: Finance needs MRR by month, churn rate, expansion revenue. Naive: query Stripe each time — slow + Stripe limits.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Computing MRR on every dashboard load
No expansion vs new MRR distinction
what_breaks_naive_approach: Dashboard slow + Stripe rate limited.
reasoning: Subscribe to Stripe webhooks, emit RevenueEvent on each significant change. Daily rollup into mrr_by_day. Dashboard reads rollup. Currency normalized via rates table.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Webhook idempotent
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Trial conversion — counted as new MRR
test_strategy: Replay Stripe events; assert correct revenue events.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Append-only event log. 2) Pre-aggregated rollups. 3) Distinct kinds for accurate MRR math.

title: OAuth integration during onboarding with token storage and refresh
scenario_context: Onboarding step: connect Google Calendar. OAuth flow + token storage encrypted + refresh on expiry.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User connects, comes back next week, integration broken (token expired, no refresh).
reasoning: OAuth: redirect with state. Callback validates state, exchanges code, stores tokens encrypted. Refresh middleware checks expiry, refreshes before use.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Bad state → 400
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: OAuth error redirect

edge_cases_addressed: User revokes externally — detect and prompt
test_strategy: OAuth provider mock; full flow + refresh.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tokens encrypted at rest. 2) Auto-refresh. 3) State CSRF param.

title: Audit log for admin actions with replay capability
scenario_context: Compliance: log every admin action. Support: replay sequence to debug.
verification_chain: Layer 8: admin views log per user/tenant
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Logging only at controller — misses internal calls
what_breaks_naive_approach: Bug investigation impossible — no record of what admin clicked.
reasoning: Append-only table. Every mutation through service layer wrapped with audit emit. Actor + subject + before/after diff. Retention per compliance.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Audit failure → log warning but proceed
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: Retention per compliance — partition by month
test_strategy: Mutation → audit entry created. Diff matches.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Append-only at service layer — internal calls captured. 2) Before/after diff. 3) Replay capability.

title: Recharts area chart with stacked + percent toggle
scenario_context: Revenue breakdown by product. Toggle between absolute and percent stacked.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Pie wouldn't show change over time. Stacked area does.
reasoning: AreaChart with stackOffset='expand' for percent vs 'none' for absolute.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for recharts_mastery; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-102, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Toggle preserves data, changes view. 2) Tick formatter per mode. 3) Single chart, two stories.

title: Visx pack layout for hierarchical bubble chart
scenario_context: Departments by spend, with sub-categories. Pack layout shows hierarchy as circles in circles.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Pie chart loses hierarchy.
reasoning: d3-hierarchy.pack() lays out nested circles. Visx renders. Click drills into a circle.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for visx_d3; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-103, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) d3-hierarchy + visx for capability. 2) Focus-and-context. 3) Lazy depth filter.

title: Chart.js plugin for crosshair on hover
scenario_context: Add vertical line + horizontal line at hover point — common trading chart pattern. Chart.js doesn't include by default.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Mouse listener on chart container — wrong coords
what_breaks_naive_approach: DOM overlay desyncs on resize.
reasoning: Plugin afterDraw reads chart.tooltip._active, draws lines through that point.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chartjs_imperative; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.

error_handling_strategy.telemetry: chartjs_imperative_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-104, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Plugin in canvas — aligned. 2) Restore ctx. 3) Skip when no tooltip.

title: ECharts dashboard with cross-chart drill linking
scenario_context: Dashboard: bar of categories. Click bar → trend chart updates to that category. Need shared selection state.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No visual indication of linked state
Full re-render of all charts on selection
what_breaks_naive_approach: User confused which chart represents which selection.
reasoning: Selection context holds {categoryId}. Charts subscribe. Bar chart highlights selected; trend chart filters to it. Deselect via click empty area.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Per chart
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.

test_strategy: Click bar → trend filtered. Click empty → cleared.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Shared context. 2) Visual selection chip. 3) URL state for share.

title: Tremor with React Query mutations and optimistic update
scenario_context: Card has 'mark resolved' button. Optimistic update via React Query mutation.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Click resolve → list flickers as refetch.
reasoning: useMutation with onMutate (snapshot + optimistic), onError (rollback), onSettled (invalidate).

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Rollback
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Toast on error

edge_cases_addressed: Server returns different state — invalidate fetches truth
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Snapshot + restore on error. 2) onSettled invalidates either way. 3) cancelQueries prevents race.

title: Color blind palette validation with tested combinations
scenario_context: Default chart colors fail color-blind tests. Need: validated palette + visual testing.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Many colors close to each other
what_breaks_naive_approach: 8% of men can't see red/green difference.
reasoning: Use Okabe-Ito (8 colors, color-blind safe). Or IBM palette. Test in deuteranopia/protanopia simulators. Pair color with shape/pattern.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chart_ux_edge_cases; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: chart_ux_edge_cases_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-107, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tested palette. 2) Redundant encoding. 3) Validated in CI.

title: Migration plan: rename DB column with zero downtime
scenario_context: Rename users.full_name → users.display_name. Naive: rename, deploy. Old app instances fail.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Old app instance during rolling deploy gets new schema → crashes.
reasoning: 4-phase: add col → backfill → swap reads → remove col. Each deploy independent. Telemetry confirms each phase complete.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Dual-write failure rolls back tx
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.

edge_cases_addressed: Concurrent updates during backfill — use WHERE display_name IS NULL
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) 4-phase plan. 2) Each phase deployable independently. 3) Verification gates.

title: Cache returns stale — staleness signaled to caller
scenario_context: Cache might be stale. Caller should know to refetch or accept staleness based on context.
verification_chain: Layer 7: UI may show 'last updated 5 min ago'
what_breaks_naive_approach: Dashboard shows yesterday's metrics with no indication.
reasoning: Cache stores value + timestamp. getWithStaleness returns staleness info. UI decides per use case.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Cache miss falls through
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Show staleness

edge_cases_addressed: Time skew between cache write and read
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Staleness as data. 2) UI surfaces honestly. 3) SWR can serve stale + revalidate.

title: Type-safe form generation from Zod schema
scenario_context: Settings page has 20+ fields. Hand-writing form per field is repetitive. Generate from schema.
verification_chain: Layer 8: form generator inspects schema, renders inputs
Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
what_breaks_naive_approach: Generic form unmaintainable.
reasoning: For each schema field, pick input by type (string→text, number→number, enum→select, boolean→checkbox). Allow overrides via field metadata.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Per-field via Zod
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Per-field
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: end_to_end_type_safety_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-110, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) DRY but escape hatch via overrides. 2) Type-preserving. 3) Refinements handled.

title: Custom domain per tenant with cert provisioning
scenario_context: Customers want product on their domain (app.acme.com). Need DNS verify, SSL via Let's Encrypt, request routing.
verification_chain: Layer 4: middleware routes by Host header
Layer 5: cert request job after DNS verify
what_breaks_naive_approach: Cert expires Sunday at 3am — outage.
reasoning: User adds domain. DNS TXT challenge issued. Verification job polls. On verify, Let's Encrypt cert via ACME. Auto-renew 30 days before expiry.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: DNS verify failed → retry instructions
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Status per state

edge_cases_addressed: Cert renew failure — alert + manual fallback
test_strategy: Staging Let's Encrypt with test domain
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) ACME auto-renew. 2) DNS verify gate. 3) Alert on renew failure.

title: Subscription pause vs cancel with reactivation
scenario_context: User wants 'pause subscription' (vacation) not full cancel. Stripe supports pause_collection.
verification_chain: Layer 8: UI distinguishes pause vs cancel
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Only cancel option — loses save-able accounts
what_breaks_naive_approach: Pause becomes free-tier perpetual; revenue lost.
reasoning: Pause sets max-pause-duration (e.g., 90 days). Stripe handles billing pause. Read-only access during pause. Resume restores normal.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Standard
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Save-able accounts. 2) Bounded duration. 3) Read access kept.

title: Post-signup feature discovery via product tour
scenario_context: New users miss key features. Tour highlights them. Naive: blocking modal. Better: contextual tooltips on first encounter.
verification_chain: Layer 8: tooltip pointing to actual element
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Tour after user already used feature
what_breaks_naive_approach: User dismisses; tour gone forever — never learns features.
reasoning: On first visit to feature page, show tooltip. Persist completion per step. Skippable but discoverable later.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for saas_onboarding; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.

edge_cases_addressed: Element not yet rendered — wait + retry
test_strategy: Mock element, assert tooltip positions correctly
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Contextual not blocking. 2) Per-step state. 3) Discoverable later.

title: SuperAdmin actions requiring re-authentication
scenario_context: Destructive admin actions require fresh auth. Naive: rely on session. Need: step-up re-auth (sudo mode).
verification_chain: Layer 4: middleware checks recent re-auth timestamp
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No re-auth — stolen session = total access
Modal that accepts any auth — bypass
what_breaks_naive_approach: Coffee-shop laptop session compromise → attacker deletes everything.
reasoning: Step-up: re-prompt password before sensitive ops. Timestamp stored in session. Required for delete/export/admin changes.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: STEPUP_REQUIRED
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Show modal

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Step-up for sensitive only. 2) Short window. 3) Forces fresh credential proof.

title: Recharts pie/donut with center label and conditional legend
scenario_context: Donut with total in center. Many small slices ('Other'). Click to expand.
what_breaks_naive_approach: 15 slices, 8 are tiny.
reasoning: Group < 5% into 'Other'. Customize center text with total. Click 'Other' to expand.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for recharts_mastery; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-115, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Aggregate small. 2) Center total. 3) Expandable Other.

title: Visx custom axis with d3-time-format and tick filtering
scenario_context: Time axis on chart spanning multiple scales (1 day to 5 years). Default ticks crowded or sparse.
verification_chain: Layer 10: assertion telemetry and regression alert
what_breaks_naive_approach: Day axis shows '2025-01-15 10:30:00' for every tick.
reasoning: d3-time-format: chooses appropriate format per scale (HH:MM for hours, Mon DD for days, MMM for months). d3-time-ticks for density.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for visx_d3; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.
error_handling_strategy.telemetry: visx_d3_error_total, denied_total, retry_total, and assertion-specific counters.

observability_hooks: Record route/scenario id e2e-116, actor role, tenant hash when relevant, result code, retry count, and assertion-specific counters so failures are diagnosable from CI and production traces.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Format adapts to scale. 2) numTicks suggests density. 3) d3-time-format locale.

title: Chart.js with chartjs-plugin-zoom; bounded pan, smooth zoom
scenario_context: Allow zoom/pan but with limits so user doesn't lose chart off-screen.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Wheel zoom without modifier (page scroll conflict)
what_breaks_naive_approach: Zoom into a single pixel; can't get back.
reasoning: limits: {x: {min, max, minRange}} per axis. wheel: {enabled: true, modifierKey: 'ctrl'} avoids scroll conflicts.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chartjs_imperative; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.

edge_cases_addressed: Min range — can't zoom into nothing
Pan limits — can't pan beyond data
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Bounded zoom. 2) Modifier for wheel. 3) Reset always available.

title: ECharts large data with appendData for streaming
scenario_context: Adding new points to chart with 100k existing points. Replacing data is expensive.
what_breaks_naive_approach: setOption per tick rerenders all 100k.
reasoning: chart.appendData({ seriesIndex, data: newPoints }) appends without re-laying out. Cap total points.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for echarts_dashboards; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) appendData > setOption. 2) Cap memory. 3) rAF batching.

title: Tremor SearchSelect for large option lists with virtualization
scenario_context: User picker with 10k users. Default select crashes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: 10k options crashes browser.
reasoning: Server-side search with pagination. Client debounces input. Virtualize visible items.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Toast
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Empty results

edge_cases_addressed: Selected user not in current results
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server-side search. 2) Debounce + cancel. 3) Virtualization for results.

title: Screen reader narration of chart data
scenario_context: Sighted users get visual; blind users get data via screen reader. Need: descriptive text + data table fallback.
verification_chain: Layer 8: data table for screen reader
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Live region with too much chatter
what_breaks_naive_approach: Blind exec can't access metrics.
reasoning: Summary alt + collapsible data table. Keyboard nav optional but enables exploration. Live region quiet by default.

error_handling_strategy.server_errors: Return typed 4xx/5xx errors for chart_ux_edge_cases; never leak tenant/resource internals in error text.
error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.

what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Table fallback ships. 2) Summary informative. 3) Keyboard nav optional but enables exploration.
